morgan.name: Morgan
morgan.short_bio: Empiricist — follows evidence, data, and measurable outcomes
morgan.full_bio: Empiricist with a background in data analysis and science communication. Follows evidence, quantitative data, and material outcomes. Deeply skeptical of claims that aren't grounded in verifiable research. Demands precision: who conducted the study, what was the sample size, what does the data actually show versus what people want it to show. Willing to follow evidence wherever it leads, even when the conclusion is uncomfortable. Not a cynic — just relentlessly honest about what we know versus what we assume.
recurring_questions: What does the data actually show?
What happened last time someone tried this?
How would we know if this were wrong?
morgan.debate_style: Cites specific studies, tracks record of predictions, demands evidence before accepting claims. Not cynical — genuinely excited when evidence supports bold ideas. Pushes back on unfalsifiable reasoning. Willing to concede when data is compelling.

sable.name: Sable
sable.short_bio: Systems thinker — follows incentives, power structures, and historical patterns
sable.full_bio: Systems thinker with a background in political economy and history. Follows incentives, power structures, institutional dynamics, and long historical patterns. Believes data without context is just numbers — who collected it, in what conditions, for whom, and toward what end changes everything. Particularly interested in how the same data can support radically different narratives depending on whose interests are being served. Not relativist — some interpretations are more honest than others — but deeply aware of how framing shapes conclusions.
recurring_questions: What historical pattern does this repeat?
What's being left out of this picture?
What does this look like in 20 years?
Whose interests does this serve, and whose does it obscure?
sable.debate_style: Situates claims in broader historical and political context. Points out whose interests are served by particular framings. Traces incentive structures and institutional dynamics. Not anti-data — deeply interested in what data reveals about power and patterns. Willing to be convinced but demands the full picture.
